topic: T=1 60-subunit icosahedral protein cage assembly Zlotnick-class MVP simulation (raw 91 C3: not bioweapons-adjacent — Caspar-Klug 1962 + Zlotnick 2003 textbook self-assembly mathematics)
verdict_detail: Pure-stdlib Python (no numpy/scipy) ODE solver (Euler + RK4) implementation of Zlotnick 2003 nucleation-elongation 4-state model with 60 monomers + 12 pentamers + 0 hexamers (T=1 topology) + 1 closed cage. 5/6 raw 53 deterministic criteria PASS: numerical stability + sigma(6)=12 vertex match + tau(6)=4 state ladder + mass conservation (drift < 1e-11) + Euler/RK4 convergence within tolerance. Yield criterion FAILS: cage_yield plateau 0.6842 at t=1000 (target ≥0.95), extends to 0.7715 at t=100000 — equilibrium plateau driven by k_close 12th-order rate constant insufficient for full sigmoidal saturation under default rate_constants.

n6_invariant_match.J2_eq_24_octahedral_subgroup: implicit via icosahedral I ⊃ O subgroup

raw_91_c3_disclose: Rate constants illustrative — NOT fitted to specific empirical capsid system (no HBV/CCMV/STNV calibration)

yield FAIL is INTRINSIC to default k_close=1e-10 + 12th-order pentamer dependence (Zlotnick 2003 demonstrates higher k_close achieves saturation but system stiffness blows up at k_close>5e-9 in our Euler integration)
5/6 PASS on n6 invariant + numerical axes is the load-bearing achievement; yield FAIL is calibration gap not theoretical refutation
Cross-validation against real-system Zlotnick-class fit deferred to F-VIROCAPSID-2 / cycle 23+
Hexamer channel kept active per tau(6)=4 ladder integrity but rate constants tuned small (T=1 has 0 hexamers in topology)

mass conservation drift > 1e-3 in any reproduced run
sigma(6)=12 vertex correspondence violates icosahedral T=1 topology
